children.value: participants. I’ll update this chapter each time a new batch of participant data is processed.
What this chapter is and isn’t

children.label: What this chapter is and isn’t

children.value: I want to use this chapter as a running results documentation, inclunding a standing summary of the analysis pipeline output as it is at the time of writing, intended as the basis for our meetings. Each section reports the effect-size estimate, its precision, and a read of how it compares to the previous batch of data. Where a coefficient was tentatively-significant at lower N and is now non-significant, that is flagged explicitly; where a finding is consolidating, that is noted; where a methodological issue has surfaced, it is described and triaged.
For the model specifications themselves, see
These are the effects whose point estimates have remained stable as N has grown from 17 to 27 and whose standard errors have decreased commensurately.
1.1 Instruction depth produces large, monotonic effects on accuracy and defense rate

children.label: 1.1 Instruction depth produces large, monotonic effects on accuracy and defense rate

children.value: The two single-coefficient effects with the strongest evidence are both main effects of
(the linear orthogonal-polynomial contrast across the three ordered instruction conditions):

β = −0.524 (SE = 0.125, p < 0.0001) on log-error scale. Compared to N = 17 (β = −0.548, SE = 0.144), the point estimate has barely moved while the SE has tightened. On the original error scale, this corresponds to roughly a 50% reduction in mean absolute angular error from condition 1 to condition 3.

posterior mean = +0.701 (posterior SD = 0.059) on logit scale. Strengthened relative to N = 17 (PM = +0.582, SD = 0.071); the posterior mean has moved upward and the SD has decreased --> bottom-line performance effect of instruction depth.
The two effects are consistent with each other (better accuracy → more mosquitoes blocked) and represent the most stable signal in the current dataset.
1.2 Within-episode consolidation is universal and substantial

children.label: 1.2 Within-episode consolidation is universal and substantial

children.value: (normalised within-episode position) main effects in M1, M3, M6, and M7 are all highly significant in the expected direction:

All four within-episode trajectories are consolidating (accuracy improves, allocations tighten, placement gets faster, and defense rate rises within an episode) --> characterises the basic learning curve (and provides face validity for the operationalisation).
Model M4 (trial-to-trial belief shift Δ_t) recovers the canonical behavioural signature of evidence-driven updating:
β = +0.949, p < 0.0001 (large belief shift after a single noisy outlier)
β = +0.696, p = 0.001 (large belief shift after an episode boundary)
β = −0.440, p = 0.001 (smaller belief shift after an uninformative trial)

contrast is approximately 1.39 log-units (corresponding to a roughly 4-fold ratio in raw Δ_t). My interpretation is that this supports the assumption that participants do approximate Bayesian updating since this effect - that surprise-with-information drives larger updates than surprise-without-information - is exactly what you would predict here.
In M3 (precision), the main effect of
β = +0.298 on logit-circ-var scale, p < 0.0001 (allocations broaden after an oddball).
This is a rational response: a participant who has just observed a possibly-anomalous outcome should hedge more on the next trial. The effect is robust and well-precision-estimated.
1.5 Cross-session meta-learning of episode shape is detectable

children.label: 1.5 Cross-session meta-learning of episode shape is detectable

children.value: M6 (placement time) reveals two stable consolidation effects and one stable interaction:
β = +0.020, p < 0.0001 (the within-episode speedup “flattens” over the session)

The interaction is logical from my perspective (I think that the within-episode speedup curve becomes less steep as participants gain experience over the session because they are already fast at the start of an episode, so there is less room to speed up further)
1.6 Post-oddball performance is depressed in defense rate

children.label: 1.6 Post-oddball performance is depressed in defense rate

children.value: PM = −0.555 (SD = 0.114) on logit scale. Defense rate drops on the trial after an oddball, because the post-oddball belief shift is partly mis-calibrated and the participant places nets less optimally for the next mosquito.
1.7 Within-episode signed bias drifts in the anticipatory direction

children.label: 1.7 Within-episode signed bias drifts in the anticipatory direction

children.value: M2 now fits with full random effects (it had collapsed to OLS at N = 17). The strongest effect:
β = +21.1, p < 0.0001 (signed error grows positively with within-episode position).

Around the centred design point, a participants signed error is approximately 0° at the start of an episode and approximately +10° at the end --> reflects that an anticipatory positive bias toward the next expected target develops within the episode (which is itself evidence of structural prediction/ inference of latent structure (rather than passive learning of the current state))
β = −5.19, p = 0.010 (a systematic cue-A vs cue-B angular offset. Expected, controlled-for, not really substantial).
Part 2 - Findings that have weakened or reversed at N = 27

children.label: Part 2 - Findings that have weakened or reversed at N = 27

children.value: These are effects that were tentative-to-significant at the pilot N = 17 and have moved toward null at N = 27.
2.1 The condition × oddball_lag1 interaction in M3 has weakened substantially

children.label: 2.1 The condition × oddball_lag1 interaction in M3 has weakened substantially

children.value: This was the cleanest focal Factor 1 finding at N = 17 (β = +0.31, p = 0.025). At N = 27 it stands at:

The point estimate has dropped by ~40% and the result is no longer significant. The direction is preserved, but the strength of evidence is materially weaker. The companion interaction in M4 (mean-shift) was already null at N = 17 (β = +0.18, p = 0.61) and is essentially zero now (β = −0.007, p = 0.98).

The “ambiguous instructions amplify dispersion-side oddball reactivity” story is not surviving the larger sample. With another 60-70 participants to come we cannot rule it out, but the credible interval at N = 27 is wide and includes zero.
2.2 The main effect of condition on precision (M3) has weakened

children.label: 2.2 The main effect of condition on precision (M3) has weakened

children.value: The point estimate has shrunk and the significance has been lost. Combined with 2.1, this means the M3 precision findings are currently the most uncertain part of the picture (as discussed in our meeting). M1 accuracy and M7 defense rate continue to show strong condition effects; M3 precision does not.
2.3 The condition × episode_index_c interaction in M1 has dropped to marginal

children.label: 2.3 The condition × episode_index_c interaction in M1 has dropped to marginal

children.value: This was the “ambiguous-instruction participants gradually catch up over the session” pattern. It is still directionally consistent and has not been overturned, but it is no longer significant at the conventional threshold and may not survive the full sample.
2.4 The latent-inference findings in M5 have weakened slightly

children.label: 2.4 The latent-inference findings in M5 have weakened slightly

children.value: The most “latent inference focused” covariate in M5 (the evidence-dependence of cross-cue generalisation) is now marginally significant. The direction is preserved and theoretically meaningful (more
₁ evidence --> smaller |I_e|) but the strength of evidence is weaker than the pilot suggested.

The intercept has also dropped from ~117° to ~107°, which shows that the average participant in the larger sample shows somewhat more generalisation at the centred design point than the pilot estimated.
remain non-significant in M5, which means that none of the condition-modulation hypotheses on M5 have empirical support at this N.
2.5 The condition × episode_index_c interaction in M7 has reversed direction

children.label: 2.5 The condition × episode_index_c interaction in M7 has reversed direction

children.value: At N = 17, the directional pattern (PM = −0.052, SD = 0.028) was loosely consistent with the M1 pattern. At N = 27, the M7 interaction is essentially zero (PM = −0.017, SD = 0.023). M1’s interaction is still numerically positive but marginal; M7’s no longer is. So the cross-model coherence of “condition modulates the across-session learning slope” has weakened.
